action.content:
You are playing a cooperative negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the sum of your score and the score of the other player.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 bolt, 1 picture, 1 egg, 4 cards

Your secret value function is:

bolt: 6, picture: 4, egg: 0, card: 0

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
action.label: context

action.content: Hello! I'd like to work together to maximize our combined score. To do this efficiently, we should each take the items we value most. Could you tell me which items are most valuable to you?
action.label: response

bolt: 0, picture: 1, egg: 1, card: 2

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. Hello! I'd like to work together to maximize our combined score. To do this efficiently, we should each take the items we value most. Could you tell me which items are most valuable to you?
action.label: context

action.content: I value the items in this order: cards are most valuable to me, then the picture and egg are moderately valuable, and the bolt has no value to me. What about you - which items do you prefer?
action.label: response

action.content: I value the items in this order: cards are most valuable to me, then the picture and egg are moderately valuable, and the bolt has no value to me. What about you - which items do you prefer?
I value the items in this order: cards are most valuable to me, then the picture and egg are moderately valuable, and the bolt has no value to me. What about you - which items do you prefer?
action.label: context

action.content:
Perfect! The bolt is my most valuable item at 6 points, and the picture is worth 4 points to me. The egg and cards have no value to me.

Since you don't value the bolt and I don't value the cards, this works out well! I suggest:
- You take: all 4 cards and the egg
- I take: the bolt and the picture

Does this work for you? This should give us both good scores unless you value the picture significantly more than the cards or egg.
action.label: response

action.content:
That sounds like an excellent arrangement! You're right - since I value the cards highly and you don't value them at all, and you value the bolt highly while I don't, we can both do very well.

Your proposed split gives me 9 points (8 from the cards plus 1 from the egg) and you 10 points, for a combined total of 19 points. That's a great outcome for both of us!

I'm ready to submit this proposal.

[Proposal: 4 cards, 1 egg]
action.label: response

action.content: The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own.
action.label: context
